name: networkx
tagline: Reference skill for creating, analyzing, and visualizing graphs/networks in Python with the NetworkX library.
verdict: Clean, low-risk skill. No credential access, network egress, shell-piped code, obfuscation, or hidden instructions found. All code examples operate on in-memory graph objects and local files the user explicitly names; nothing destructive or irreversible runs without the user driving it. Only minor quality gaps: no explicit negative triggers and thin failure-

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance ('do not use for X') anywhere in the file; a user can't easily tell when NOT to reach for this skill (e.g. non-graph tabular analysis).

Failure-path handling is thin: install guidance only checks `nx.__version__` (lines 261-263) without handling ImportError, and no notes on common runtime errors (e.g. disconnected-graph exceptions fro
